g.data-narration: Introducing Swing in Java - a comprehensive guide to building desktop GUI applications.
Swing is a GUI toolkit in Java for building desktop applications, part of the Java Foundation Classes.
Swing is lightweight, platform-independent, uses MVC architecture, and has pluggable look and feel.
AWT uses heavyweight native components while Swing uses pure Java lightweight components for platform independence.
Swing follows MVC architecture: Controller handles events, Model stores data, and View displays the GUI.
The Swing hierarchy extends from Object to Component, Container, JComponent, then to JFrame, JDialog, and all UI components.
Top-level containers include JFrame for main windows, JDialog for popups, and JApplet for deprecated web content.
Common Swing components include JLabel, JButton, JTextField, JTextArea, JCheckBox, JRadioButton, and JComboBox.
g.text: JLabel lbl = new JLabel("Hello"); JButton btn = new JButton("Click"); JTextField tf = new JTextField(20);

g.data-narration: Layout managers control component positioning: FlowLayout wraps items, BorderLayout divides into five regions, GridLayout creates grids.
g.text: 5 regions: N, S, E, W, C

g.data-narration: Swing uses the Delegation Event Model with ActionListener, MouseListener, and KeyListener to handle user interactions.
g.text: Swing uses Delegation Event Model where listeners handle events.

g.data-narration: Create a JFrame by setting size, default close operation, centering it on screen, and making it visible.
Best practices include using invokeLater for thread safety, separating GUI from logic, handling exceptions, and using layout managers.
Remember key exam points: JFrame hierarchy, event listeners, layout managers, thread safety with EDT, and EXIT_ON_CLOSE.
g.text: 4. Thread Safety: Swing not thread-safe; use EDT

g.data-narration: In summary, Swing is a lightweight, platform-independent GUI toolkit with rich components and event-driven architecture.
Master Swing fundamentals to build professional Java desktop applications with rich, platform-independent GUIs.
g.text: Master Swing fundamentals to build professional Java applications!
